ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ e kaŋɛ anya na to be emo nɛ e daŋ juŋkpar na fanɛ mo da Isɔɔ baŋ tu mo nseŋ bishi mo fanɛ, “Wanɛ be esa e la fo? Nnɛ nɛ fee yɔ? Wanɛ e wɔ asɔbɔya damta nɛ b juŋkpar fo ere kikɛ?”
translated: When the Gonjas heard that he had arrived, they gathered together and sent a message to Issah asking him why he had come. They said, "What have we done to you? Why are you here? What offense have we committed that you have come to attack us?"

ref: Seir was from the Horite tribe that had lived in Edom before the time of Esau. The clans that had descended from him were Lotan, Shobal, Zibeon, Anah,
text: Seyir nɛ e la Horaiebi be esa na be mbinyɛnbi nɛ b daa wɔ efuli na so nde. Lotan nɛ Shobal nɛ Zibiɔn nɛ Ana,
translated: These are the sons of Seir the Horite, the inhabitants of the land: Lotan and Shobal and Zibeon and Anah,

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ebɔrɛ nɛfa bumo nseŋ kaŋɛ bumo le: “Men kurge n wora keshi n salga to n sɔ kasawule so kikɛ nsaa ji eleŋ kumo so. Men baa ji eleŋ ekɔrɔtɔ so nɛ mbuibi so nɛ asɔ gbeyeso kikɛ nyam so.”
translated: When the Lord God created the world, he said to the world: "I have made you and given you a day of rest which is called Sunday. I have also given you a moon which is called Monday and a star which is called Saturday."

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: nseŋ lara nyiŋgbeŋ ashi kuloŋgboŋ na to n lɛ kowu nɛ k baa wɔtɔ a lɛa a lɛa a jo saŋɛ nɛ nchu na kikɛ nyam beeŋ feeto kasawule so kuraa.
translated: I am telling you this because of the way we are suffering here. We don't have anything to do, and even if we try to do something, we don't have the means to do it. We are just suffering and enduring.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fanɛ ma asheŋ bɔlɔ fo kenishi, ŋkpal manɛ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m mɔlga ma ŋkpa, ama abee na ase wɔ kufɔ ga. Amoso pɔɛŋ nɛ n fo ndoŋ kusɔ ko beeŋ tiŋ n wora ma nɛ n wu.
translated: I am going to the farm today to harvest yams, if I get them, I will bring some home for you to eat, but if there are none, I will return empty-handed. This is the reason why I am telling you beforehand so you are not surprised when I come.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.

translated: When the Gonja man reached there and saw that they had gathered together to eat and he also saw Issah's mother among them, he asked, "What is this? What is going on here? Why are you all gathered here eating without me?"

translated: God told his people saying: "I am the one who brought you out of the land of Egypt where you were slaves. I am the one who made a covenant with your fathers that I will give you a land flowing with milk and honey."

translated: "Then God said to them: "I give you every seed-bearing plant on the face of the whole earth and every tree that has fruit with seed in it. They will be yours for food. And to all the beasts of the earth and all the birds in the sky and all the creatures that move along the ground—everything that has the breath of life in it—I give every green plant for food."

translated: I am telling you that if you don't take care of your child and he/she grows up, he/she will not respect anyone, and that is why I am advising you to take good care of your child.

translated: If you had come to help me carry the load, you would have seen that I am not carrying anything, but there is something in the room. However, the reason why I called you is because I have a problem.

translated: When the Lord God created the world he said to the world: "I have made you and placed you on a firm foundation so that you may stand forever. I have also made the sun, the moon, and the stars so that they may give light to the earth and the sky."

ref: God gave them his blessing and said: Have a lot of children! Fill the earth with people and bring it under your control. Rule over the fish in the ocean, the birds in the sky, and every animal on the earth.
text: Ndoŋ nna nɛ Ebɔrɛ nɛfa bumo nseŋ kaŋɛ bumo le: “Men kurge n wora keshi n salga to n sɔ kasawule so kikɛ nsaa ji eleŋ kumo so. Men baa ji eleŋ ekɔrɔtɔ so nɛ mbuibi so nɛ asɔ gbeyeso kikɛ nyam so.”
translated: My father and Ebo said that a bird does not forget its beak: "I am a small creature but I walk on the lion's head. I am also on the lion's back and on the leopard's skin and on the elephant's skin."

ref: Forty days later Noah opened a window to send out a raven, but it kept flying around until the water had dried up.
text: nseŋ lara nyiŋgbeŋ ashi kuloŋgboŋ na to n lɛ kowu nɛ k baa wɔtɔ a lɛa a lɛa a jo saŋɛ nɛ nchu na kikɛ nyam beeŋ feeto kasawule so kuraa.
translated: If you don't have a hoe and cutlass, you can't farm. And if you don't farm, you won't get food to eat. That is why I am advising you to take good care of your farming tools.

ref: Lot answered, “You have done us a great favor, sir. You have saved our lives, but please don't make us go to the hills. That's too far away. The city will be destroyed before we can get there, and we will be killed when it happens.
text: N wu fanɛ ma asheŋ bɔlɔ fo kenishi, ŋkpal manɛ so, fo wora ma kelela ga m mɔlga ma ŋkpa, ama abee na ase wɔ kufɔ ga. Amoso pɔɛŋ nɛ n fo ndoŋ kusɔ ko beeŋ tiŋ n wora ma nɛ n wu.
translated: I am going to the farm today to harvest yams, but if I don't get any, I will not return home until I find something, because we have nothing at home to eat. That is why I left home early this morning before my wife woke up.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.
text: Kumo be kaman nɛ e kaŋɛ anya na to be emo nɛ e daŋ juŋkpar na fanɛ mo da Isɔɔ baŋ tu mo nseŋ bishi mo fanɛ, “Wanɛ be esa e la fo? Nnɛ nɛ fee yɔ? Wanɛ e wɔ asɔbɔya damta nɛ b juŋkpar fo ere kikɛ?”
translated: When the Gonja man reached there, he saw that the old woman was grinding pepper, and he asked her, "What is your name? Where are you going? What kind of work are you doing that you are grinding pepper like this?"
